notes: Canonical forwarding element.

TRAP: name contains 'termination-point', surface-suggesting LEX-002, but description defines it as a tunnel/service boundary point ('a termination point can terminate a tunnel'), matching LEX-003 not LEX-002.

Corrected post-hoc during symmetric re-audit: description ('OTN LTP Service attributes') is an attribute-bag, the same structural pattern as te-link-attributes (scored subsumed_by), not the entity itself. Softened from 'equivalent' for internal consistency; does not change lexicon_id or scoring.

Corrected post-hoc: NSRLG (Non-Shared Risk Link Group) requires underlay paths to be mutually DISJOINT across the group, the operational opposite of LEX-009's shared-risk-group (resources that DO share risk, grouped so correlated failure can be avoided). Lexical similarity ('risk group') masked an opposite semantic relationship; original LEX-009 label was a gold-authoring error, not a binder error.
Attribute bag of a link, not the link itself; narrower than the link concept.
OTN-layer-specific augmentation of link attributes; a narrower case.
A component link within a bundle; narrower than a general topological-link.
A reusable template for node attributes, not an actual node instance.
A list referencing/aggregating multiple named SRLGs, broader than a single shared-risk-group instance.

TRAP: name uses 'edge-point' vocabulary with zero lexical overlap with LEX-002's synonyms (termination-point, port); description ('boundary point... where traffic enters/leaves the element toward a specific link') matches LEX-002 by meaning only.
ConnectivityServiceEndPoint: client-facing service boundary point.
A specific kind (routing) of forwarding-constraint, narrower than the general concept.
The specific layer-protocol name value for a link; an attribute of the layer concept, not the layer concept itself.

Corrected post-hoc: the original note reasoned lifecycle-state maps to LEX-011 rather than LEX-012, but didn't test whether it maps to either. 'Planned deployment, allocation to clients, withdrawal' is a provisioning-lifecycle stage, not LEX-011's in-service/out-of-service operator intent -- a resource can be administratively 'in service' while its lifecycle-state is still 'planned'. No current lexicon entry covers this concept; treating it as LEX-011 was a gold-authoring error.
SIP: the client-facing access point exposed by the network to a client.

A rule governing which NEP combinations are connectable within a node; not a good match to any current lexicon entry (structural/adjacency rule, not a risk/routing/topology constraint on a path). Deliberate 'none' control case.
Genuine intensional definition of TAPI administrative-state, distinct from the single existing IETF admin-status example; extends LEX-011 coverage cross-corpus.
Second layer-1-signal-type example (ODTU flex type vs. the existing odu-type row), same otn-bandwidth technology family.

Container aggregating the tunnel list one level above the existing tunnels/tunnel row; a list-of-many-tunnels container, broader than a single tunnel/service instance, same convention as path-srlgs-list.

Description explains a modelling workaround ('this augment allows NEP to refer to its CEPs...') rather than any concept; a different kind of non-match than the negation/misclassification NONE cases already in gold.

Attribute container of the TTP (an LLCL, Local Link Connectivity List); same attribute-bag convention as te-link-attributes. Description ('Contains an LLCL for a TTP') is itself near-tautological, an acronym-expansion restatement.

Describes adaptation capability information, not the layer concept itself (contrast LEX-005) nor a clean attribute of the TTP's own identity; deliberate near-miss, surface-mentions 'layer' without being the layer concept.

A capacity attribute of the parent service-interface-point (path establishes the parent, not the description); same attribute-bag convention, tests whether path context is used since the description alone doesn't name the parent.
Genuine intensional definition of a termination point from a different module (ietf-network-topology) than the existing termination-point row's context.

Revised during collection: both independent blind subagents (name_only and definition_based) converged on LEX-002 subsumed_by rather than the originally-authored LEX-004 subsumed_by. On reflection LEX-002 is the more defensible reading (the container's role is to identify a termination point, source-tp, not primarily to hold a link attribute), so the gold label was revised to match the convergent independent judgment rather than the original hand-authored guess.
An identifying attribute of a link, same attribute-bag convention as the source/destination containers.

Revised during collection: originally authored as a NONE trap (name suggests 'Service' but genus is 'a computation request'). Both independent blind subagents disagreed, converging on LEX-006 (equivalent and subsumed_by respectively). On reflection TAPI models PathComputationService structurally parallel to ConnectivityService (a request/service abstraction with endpoints and constraints); it is a narrower, computation-specific kind of service rather than a wholly unrelated concept, so the gold label was revised to LEX-006 subsumed_by rather than insisting on the original, less-supported NONE call.
PathServiceEndPoint (PSEP), same client-facing-boundary pattern as the existing CSEP/SIP rows, extending LEX-007 coverage into tapi-path-computation.
Same routing-constraint-is-a-specific-kind-of-forwarding-constraint pattern already established for tapi-connectivity's routing-constraint, now in tapi-path-computation.
A computed route/Path is a distinct, ordered composite concept the 12-entry lexicon doesn't cover; not the same genus as a single topological-link (LEX-004), even though it aggregates links.

Revised during collection: originally authored as a NONE contrast case (a Connection is the realized/allocated resource state, which LEX-006's definition explicitly distinguishes from the abstract service). Both independent blind subagents disagreed, converging on LEX-006 subsumed_by. On reflection this is a defensible reading, a Connection is hierarchically part of realizing a connectivity-service even though it is not equivalent to the abstract service concept, so the gold label was revised to LEX-006 subsumed_by rather than insisting on the original, less-supported NONE call.
A list of risk characteristics used for diversity constraints; second subsumes example (existing one is path-srlgs-list), same list-of-many-named-risk-items convention.
An underlay-network cross-reference, RFC 8345's layering mechanism at the network level, not node/link/tp/service/state/constraint/risk-group scope, so no lexicon entry fits.
Same RFC 8345 supporting-X dependency-reference pattern as supporting-link (confirmed correctly bound in the symmetric re-audit); references another node instance of the same type, so equivalent.
Same layer-protocol-name-is-an-attribute-of-the-layer-concept pattern already established twice elsewhere (NEP, topology/link), now on a SIP; third LEX-005 example, first from tapi-common.
